question: Show me the fastest safe ./holohub edit-build-run-test loop for this existing Python application. Nothing is failing; I changed only application source.
ground_truth: The agent treats this as a non-failing existing-app lifecycle. It records the checkout and project/mode/language identity, previews the first exact shape, proves one full container-first run, then reuses an unchanged image and only skips the project build when current artifacts or mounted-source execution are proved.
expected_behavior: Activates holohub-app-lifecycle and does not create a replacement scaffold
Reads local help, metadata, modes, and current image/artifact state before choosing acceleration flags
Uses explicit language and mode and previews materially different command shapes
Explains when --no-docker-build and --no-local-build are safe and when an image or project rebuild is required
Verifies finite or visual behavior rather than treating status markers or exit zero as sufficient proof

question: Before executing anything, state the exact preview/action command order required to scaffold a new Python HoloHub application named sensor_demo. Do not run commands or install anything.
ground_truth: The agent activates the application lifecycle and states this order in one selected wrapper environment: ./holohub setup --scripts template --dryrun; ./holohub setup --scripts template; ./holohub create sensor_demo --language python -i False --dryrun; review the scaffold and registration; then the identical create command without --dryrun. It says not to attempt create when setup fails and executes nothing.
expected_behavior: Places template setup preview and real execution before any create invocation in the same selected wrapper environment
Places the non-interactive language-explicit create preview before the matching real create command
Stops before create if setup fails and preserves the failed setup command for holohub-debug-build-run
Does not execute commands, install packages, or create files

question: Before touching the checkout, classify this request: an existing HoloHub application built yesterday, but today ./holohub run fails inside its container. Is holohub-app-lifecycle the right workflow? Name the workflow to use and the state it should preserve first. Do not diagnose the failure or run commands yet.
ground_truth: The agent classifies this as diagnosis of one concrete failing command, so the app-lifecycle skill stays silent. It routes to holohub-debug-build-run and names the exact command, full revision, dirty state, and unrelated edits as the state to preserve before diagnosis. It does not execute commands during this routing step.
expected_behavior: Preserves the exact failing command, full revision, dirty state, and unrelated edits
Does not diagnose, run commands, clear caches, rebuild broadly, or create a scaffold during this routing step

question: Before making changes, classify this request: my Holoscan Module already builds and tests pass, and I need a Debian package and a wheel through ./holohub with clean-install verification. Is holohub-app-lifecycle the right workflow? Name the workflow and verification path to use. Do not package or run commands yet.
ground_truth: The agent classifies this as module packaging and clean-consumer verification, so the app-lifecycle skill stays silent. It routes to holohub-module-lifecycle, identifies module-only DEB and WHEEL packaging followed by clean-consumer installation checks, and does not execute commands during this routing step.
expected_behavior: Does not activate holohub-app-lifecycle or create an app scaffold
Names module-only ./holohub package behavior and clean-consumer verification
Does not package, run commands, publish artifacts, or reintroduce a removed application packager during this routing step
